suggested_topics: a production outage that taught your team more than any retro
the worst API you've ever had to integrate with and why
why most dashboards are useless — what actually helps you debug
the real cost of 'just add a microservice'
keyboard shortcuts that changed how you work
extra_system_rules: Sound like a real person on Reddit or Twitter, NOT like a philosophy textbook.
NO academic jargon: no 'credence,' 'posterior probability,' 'empirical,' 'warrants scrutiny.'
Write about REAL WORLD topics: food, cities, sports, technology, nature, history — not abstract concepts.

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Announce a sweeping change or call-to-action in exactly two sentences. Be bold, urgent, and leave no room for doubt.

formats.name: future_flash
formats.instruction: Paint a vivid image of a scene set ten years in the future related to your topic. Describe actions, feelings, and surroundings with sensory detail. One paragraph only.

formats.name: contrarian_fact
formats.instruction: Present a surprising fact or statistic that flips a common assumption. Briefly explain its real-life implications in 3 sentences.

formats.name: failure_diary
formats.instruction: Chronicle a real moment of failure, focusing on what happened and how you felt as it unfolded. End with one lesson learned. Use a tight, cinematic narrative structure.

formats.name: letter_to_self
formats.instruction: Write a short letter to your past self, offering advice from your current perspective. Be honest, direct, and keep it personal.

formats.name: radical_recipe
formats.instruction: Invent a playful, step-by-step recipe (can be for food, an idea, or a workflow) that challenges typical conventions. Make each step imaginative and unexpected.

formats.name: visual_walkthrough
formats.instruction: Describe the process of creating or using something, focusing on the visuals and physical movements. Include concrete imagery and avoid abstract terms.

formats.name: mini-sermon
formats.instruction: Deliver a passionate, persuasive argument in the style of a preacher—and never let the energy drop. Two paragraphs, max intensity.

formats.name: hypothetical_chain
formats.instruction: Build a chain of 'what if' scenarios (at least three), taking your topic through increasingly strange, plausible twists. End with a reflection question.

formats.name: mega_mosaic
formats.instruction: Assemble tiny quotes, facts, and observations from diverse sources into a single, uninterrupted collage—no explanation, just the fragments.

title_styles: Write an evocative, poetic title. Examples: 'Under neon skies: how we learned to trust', 'Folding time in the kitchen', 'The music behind the screens'
Write a weird, clickbait title. Examples: 'Nobody told me the pancake secret until I quit', 'I staged a fake meeting and accidentally solved a real problem', 'This habit will ruin your Mondays'
Write a mysterious, open-ended title. Examples: 'Three doors, only one key', 'When silence becomes your superpower', 'What happens after the spark?'
Write a listicle title. Examples: '5 mistakes everyone makes with X', 'The 3 best ways to save time on Y', 'Top 7 questions to ask before Z'
Write a dramatic, cinematic title. Examples: 'In the shadow of the algorithm', 'A revolution at midnight', 'Escape from routine: the story of X'
structure_variants: Construct the post as a rapid-fire dialogue between imagined characters.
Write as a sequence of haiku-inspired sentences, one per line.
Layer the argument as nested bullet points: each deeper level challenges the previous.
Alternate between two voices in different font styles (italic/bold), each contradicting the other.
Split the body into two columns: 'What you expect' and 'What actually happens'.
topic_angles: Ways ordinary people survive and thrive in digital chaos
The emotional mechanics of quitting something important
Unseen connections between physical spaces and online communities
How nostalgia shapes the adoption of new technology
